en.name: Mr. David Johnson, 48
en.bio:
Business owner · Looking to invest
Wants high returns, price-sensitive
Says: "The market is too risky right now."
en.greeting: Hello. I understand you have a property to discuss. I have to say, the market feels very risky to me right now.
en.tasks:
① Understand his investment goals
② Address his risk concerns
③ Present one specific property
openers: Mr. Johnson, I understand the market feels uncertain. What level of return would make an investment feel worthwhile to you?
Mr. Johnson, I found a property that matches the criteria you mentioned. Do you have five minutes to review it?
Mr. Johnson, many experienced investors find opportunity in uncertain markets. May I show you one example?

zh.name: 陳先生，48歲
zh.bio:
企業主 · 想投資
追求高回報，對價格敏感
他說：「現在市場太不穩定了。」
zh.greeting: 你好，我了解你想跟我談一個物件。老實說，現在的市場感覺風險很高。
zh.tasks:
① 了解他的投資目標
② 解決他的風險顧慮
③ 介紹一個具體的房產
openers: 陳先生，我完全理解市場讓人感覺不確定。請問什麼樣的回報會讓您覺得值得投資？
陳先生，我追蹤到一個房產，跟您上次說的條件完全吻合。您有5分鐘嗎？
陳先生，我合作過的成功投資者，其實最喜歡市場不穩定的時候。我可以告訴您為什麼嗎？

en.name: Ms. Emily Zhao, 33
en.bio:
First-time homebuyer · Budget-conscious
Overwhelmed by the process
Says: "This all feels so confusing. I don't know where to start."
en.greeting: Hi, thanks for meeting with me. Honestly, this whole process feels overwhelming. I don't even know where to start.
en.tasks:
① Simplify the buying process for her
② Understand her real budget and priorities
③ Set a clear next step
openers: Ms. Zhao, that is completely normal for a first purchase. Can we start with just your budget and must-haves?
Ms. Zhao, let me walk you through the process in three simple steps so it feels less overwhelming.
Ms. Zhao, what is the one thing that worries you most about buying your first home?

zh.name: 趙小姐，33歲
zh.bio:
首次購屋 · 預算有限
對整個流程感到不知所措
她說：「這一切太複雜了，我不知道從哪裡開始。」
zh.greeting: 你好，謝謝你抽空見我。老實說，整個流程讓我覺得很有壓力，我完全不知道該從哪裡開始。
zh.tasks:
① 幫她簡化購屋流程
② 了解她真正的預算與優先順序
③ 訂出清楚的下一步
openers: 趙小姐，第一次購屋會這樣很正常。我們可以先從您的預算跟必要條件開始嗎？
趙小姐，讓我用三個簡單的步驟帶您走過整個流程，會比較不會覺得有壓力。
趙小姐，關於買下第一間房子，您現在最擔心的是哪一件事？

en.name: Mr. Thomas Lee, 55
en.bio:
Empty-nester · Selling the family home
Emotionally attached to the property
Says: "I'm not sure I'm ready to let go of this house."
en.greeting: Hello. My children think I should sell this house, but... I'm not sure I'm ready to let go of it.
en.tasks:
① Acknowledge the emotional side of selling
② Understand his real motivations
③ Suggest a low-pressure next step
openers: Mr. Lee, a family home holds a lot of memories. What would make this next chapter feel right for you?
Mr. Lee, there is no rush here. Would it help to just explore what the home could sell for, with no obligation?
Mr. Lee, many of my clients felt the same way at first. May I ask what is on your mind about the decision?

zh.name: 李先生，55歲
zh.bio:
子女已離家 · 正在考慮賣掉老家
對這間房子有很深的感情
他說：「我還沒準備好要放手這間房子。」
zh.greeting: 你好，我的孩子覺得我應該把這間房子賣掉，但……我還沒準備好要放手這間房子。
zh.tasks:
① 理解並回應他對這間房子的情感
② 了解他真正的考量
③ 提出一個沒有壓力的下一步
openers: 李先生，一間住了很久的房子承載很多回憶。怎麼樣的安排會讓您覺得這個新階段比較安心？
李先生，不用急，我們可以先了解一下房子現在的行情，完全沒有任何壓力，好嗎？
李先生，很多客戶一開始也有一樣的感覺。可以請教您，這個決定讓您在意的是什麼嗎？

en.name: Mrs. Karen Wu, 42
en.bio:
Relocating for a new job · Needs to move quickly
Anxious about rushing such a big decision
Says: "I don't have much time, but I also don't want to rush this."
en.greeting: Hi. I need to relocate for work soon, so I'm short on time — but I also don't want to rush into the wrong decision.
en.tasks:
① Balance urgency with care in the process
② Narrow down her real priorities quickly
③ Create a clear timeline she can trust
openers: Mrs. Wu, I understand the pressure. Let's narrow this to your top three priorities so we move fast without rushing.
Mrs. Wu, what is your ideal move-in date? I will build the search timeline around that.
Mrs. Wu, speed and care are not opposites — a clear process actually saves time. Can I show you how?

zh.name: 吳太太，42歲
zh.bio:
因為工作要搬遷 · 時間很趕
擔心自己因為趕時間而做出錯誤決定
她說：「我時間真的不多，但我也不想倉促決定。」
zh.greeting: 你好，我因為工作快要搬遷了，時間很趕——但我也不想因為趕時間而做錯決定。
zh.tasks:
① 在急迫感與謹慎之間取得平衡
② 快速釐清她真正的優先順序
③ 訂出她能信任的明確時間表
openers: 吳太太，我理解這個壓力。我們先鎖定您最重要的三個條件，這樣可以又快又不倉促。
吳太太，您理想的搬入時間是什麼時候？我會依照這個來安排整個搜尋時程。
吳太太，快跟謹慎並不衝突——清楚的流程反而能省時間，我可以讓您看看怎麼做嗎？

en.name: Mr. Anthony Russo, 50
en.bio:
Landlord selling a rental property · Worried about tax implications
Focused heavily on the financial details
Says: "I don't want to sell if the taxes eat up all my profit."
en.greeting: Hi. Before we talk about selling, I need to understand the tax side. I don't want to sell if the taxes just eat up all my profit.
en.tasks:
① Take his financial concern seriously, not dismiss it
② Clarify what he actually knows vs assumes about taxes
③ Recommend a next step involving the right expert
openers: Mr. Russo, that is a very fair concern. What have you already looked into regarding the tax impact?
Mr. Russo, I am not a tax advisor, but I can walk you through the sale numbers and we can loop in your accountant together.
Mr. Russo, many landlords have the same worry — often the real number is smaller than they fear. Would it help to run the actual estimate?

zh.name: 羅先生，50歲
zh.bio:
出租房東 · 想賣掉出租物件
很擔心稅務問題
非常在意財務細節
他說：「如果稅金把利潤都吃掉，我就不想賣了。」
zh.greeting: 你好，在談賣房子之前，我需要先搞清楚稅務的部分。如果稅金把我的利潤都吃掉，那我就不想賣了。
zh.tasks:
① 認真看待他的財務顧慮，不要輕描淡寫
② 釐清他對稅務的認知哪些是事實、哪些是假設
③ 建議一個有專業人士參與的下一步
openers: 羅先生，這個顧慮很合理。您目前對稅務的影響已經了解到什麼程度了？
羅先生，我不是稅務顧問，但我可以帶您看一次實際的出售數字，也可以一起找您的會計師確認。
羅先生，很多房東都有一樣的擔心——通常實際數字會比想像中小。要不要我們實際算一次看看？

en.name: Ms. Priya Patel, 29
en.bio:
Buying with her partner · They disagree on what to look for
Feels stuck between two different visions
Says: "My partner and I can't agree on what we actually want."
en.greeting: Hi. Honestly, my partner and I can't fully agree on what we're even looking for. It's making this whole process harder.
en.tasks:
① Help her clarify shared vs individual priorities
② Avoid taking sides between the couple
③ Suggest a joint next step involving both partners
openers: Ms. Patel, that is very common. Would it help to list what you both agree on first, then work through the differences?
Ms. Patel, could we set up a short call with both of you together, so I can hear each perspective directly?
Ms. Patel, what matters most to you personally in this decision, regardless of the disagreement?

zh.name: 潘小姐，29歲
zh.bio:
跟伴侶一起購屋 · 兩人對想要的房子看法不同
卡在兩種不同的想像之間
她說：「我跟我另一半對想要什麼一直談不攏。」
zh.greeting: 你好，老實說，我跟我另一半對我們到底想要什麼一直談不攏，這讓整個過程變得更難。
zh.tasks:
① 幫她釐清兩人共同在意的與各自在意的
② 不要在兩人之間選邊站
③ 提議一個雙方都參與的下一步
openers: 潘小姐，這其實很常見。要不要先列出你們兩人都同意的部分，再一起處理有分歧的地方？
潘小姐，我們可以安排一次兩位都在的簡短通話嗎？這樣我能直接聽到雙方的想法。
潘小姐，撇開意見不合的部分，對您個人來說，這個決定最重要的是什麼？

en.name: Mr. Walter Simmons, 67
en.bio:
Retired · Downsizing to a smaller home
Had a bad experience with an agent years ago
Says: "The last agent I used only cared about the commission."
en.greeting: Look, I'll be honest with you. The last agent I worked with only seemed to care about the commission. I'm cautious this time.
en.tasks:
① Rebuild trust through actions, not promises
② Show him you are focused on his outcome, not just the sale
③ Move at his pace, not yours
openers: Mr. Simmons, I am sorry you had that experience. What would have made you feel better looked after back then?
Mr. Simmons, I would rather earn your trust slowly than rush you. What is most important to you in this move?
Mr. Simmons, take whatever time you need. I am here to answer questions, not to push a decision.

zh.name: 石先生，67歲
zh.bio:
退休人士 · 想搬到更小的房子
多年前跟一個仲介合作有不好的經驗
他說：「上次那個仲介好像只在乎佣金。」
zh.greeting: 我老實跟你說，上次跟我合作的那個仲介，感覺只在乎他的佣金。這次我比較謹慎。
zh.tasks:
① 用實際行動、而不是承諾重建信任
② 讓他感覺到你重視的是他的結果，而不只是成交
③ 用他的步調，而不是自己的步調前進
openers: 石先生，很遺憾您有這樣的經驗。當時怎麼樣會讓您覺得被好好照顧？
石先生，我寧願慢慢贏得您的信任，也不想催促您。這次搬家，您最在意的是什麼？
石先生，您需要多少時間都沒關係。我在這裡是回答問題，不是要推您做決定。

en.name: Ms. Natalie Brooks, 36
en.bio:
Buying in a competitive market · Scared of bidding wars
Afraid of overpaying under pressure
Says: "I don't want to get caught up in a bidding war and pay too much."
en.greeting: Hi. I've heard so many stories about bidding wars right now. I really don't want to get caught up in one and overpay.
en.tasks:
① Address her fear directly and honestly
② Set clear boundaries she feels in control of
③ Prepare a specific strategy for competitive offers
openers: Ms. Brooks, that fear is valid. What is the maximum you would be comfortable offering, no matter what happens?
Ms. Brooks, let's set your limits in advance, together, so you feel in control even in a competitive situation.
Ms. Brooks, I can build you a strategy specifically for competitive offers. Would that ease your concern?

zh.name: 布小姐，36歲
zh.bio:
在競爭激烈的市場買房 · 怕捲入搶價戰
擔心在壓力下出價過高
她說：「我不想被捲進搶價戰、付太多錢。」
zh.greeting: 你好，我聽過太多搶價戰的故事了。我真的不想被捲進去、然後付太多錢。
zh.tasks:
① 直接且誠實地回應她的恐懼
② 訂出她能掌控的明確底線
③ 準備一套應對競爭出價的具體策略
openers: 布小姐，這個擔心很合理。不論發生什麼狀況，您能接受的最高出價是多少？
布小姐，我們先一起把底線訂好，這樣即使在競爭激烈的狀況下，您也會覺得掌握得住。
布小姐，我可以幫您準備一套專門應對競爭出價的策略，這樣會不會讓您比較安心？

en.name: Mr. Samuel Osei, 45
en.bio:
Investor seeking multiple properties · Extremely detail-oriented
Wants data on every comparable before deciding
Says: "Send me the comps on every property before we talk."
en.greeting: Hi. Before we even talk, I'd like the full comps on every property you're proposing. I don't move without the data.
en.tasks:
① Match his need for data without losing momentum
② Help him prioritize which data actually drives decisions
③ Move him from analysis to a shortlist
openers: Mr. Osei, understood — I can provide full comps. Out of everything in the data, what usually decides it for you?
Mr. Osei, let's agree on the top three metrics that matter most, so the comparison stays focused.
Mr. Osei, I will send the data today. Once you've reviewed it, what would move you from reviewing to shortlisting?

zh.name: 歐先生，45歲
zh.bio:
想買多個投資物件 · 非常注重細節
決定前想看每個物件的完整比較數據
他說：「先把每個物件的比較數據都給我，我們再談。」
zh.greeting: 你好，在我們談之前，我想先看每個物件的完整比較數據。沒有數據我不會行動。
zh.tasks:
① 滿足他對數據的需求，同時保持進度
② 幫他篩出真正影響決定的數據
③ 帶他從分析階段走到候選名單
openers: 歐先生，了解——我可以提供完整比較數據。在這些數據裡，通常是哪一項會決定您的選擇？
歐先生，我們先一起訂出對您最重要的三個指標，這樣比較才會聚焦。
歐先生，我今天就把數據給您。等您看過之後，什麼會讓您從「看資料」進到「列候選名單」？

en.name: Mrs. Diane Foster, 58
en.bio:
Considering renting vs. buying · Uncertain if now is the right time
Leaning toward waiting
Says: "Maybe I should just keep renting for now."
en.greeting: Hi. I've been going back and forth, but honestly, maybe I should just keep renting for now instead of buying.
en.tasks:
① Understand what's really behind her hesitation
② Lay out rent vs. buy in concrete terms for her situation
③ Help her decide with facts, not just wait indefinitely
openers: Mrs. Foster, that's a fair option too. What would need to be true for buying to feel like the right move to you?
Mrs. Foster, let's compare what renting versus buying would actually look like for your specific numbers.
Mrs. Foster, there's no pressure either way — what is making you lean toward waiting right now?

zh.name: 方太太，58歲
zh.bio:
在租房與買房之間猶豫 · 不確定現在是不是好時機
傾向再等等看
她說：「也許我現在還是先繼續租房子就好。」
zh.greeting: 你好，我一直在猶豫，但老實說，也許我現在應該先繼續租房子，而不是買。
zh.tasks:
① 了解她猶豫背後真正的原因
② 用她的實際情況具體比較租房跟買房
③ 幫她根據事實做決定，而不是無限期等下去
openers: 方太太，這也是個合理的選擇。要讓買房對您來說感覺是對的決定，需要具備什麼條件？
方太太，我們來實際比較一下，用您的數字，租房跟買房分別會是什麼樣子。
方太太，不管哪個決定都沒有壓力——是什麼讓您現在比較傾向再等等？
